description: Mapping of GLBA Safeguards Rule to NIST 800-53 Rev 5

source.disclaimer: These mappings are interpretive guidance for compliance research. They are NOT official FTC or NIST crosswalks. Financial institutions should consult their primary federal regulator (OCC, FDIC, Federal Reserve, NCUA, SEC) and legal counsel for authoritative guidance.
references: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 - Security and Privacy Controls
source.methodology: Mappings based on GLBA Safeguards Rule elements (314.4) and alignment with NIST 800-53 control families. GLBA focuses on protecting customer information confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

mappings.rationale: GLBA requires designated qualified individual to oversee information security program
GLBA explicitly requires risk assessment of customer information systems
GLBA requires multi-factor authentication for information systems access
GLBA requires secure disposal of customer information
Secure and resilient customer information systems
GLBA requires continuous monitoring and logging
GLBA requires annual penetration testing or vulnerability assessments
Annual testing requirement includes vulnerability assessments
GLBA requires procedures for detecting and responding to security events
GLBA requires response program for security events affecting customer information
GLBA requires due diligence and oversight of service providers
Annual reporting to Board on security program status
